questions.question_text: Which of the following animals is a herbivore?
questions.explanation: A cow feeds mainly on plants and plant products, so it is a herbivore. Lions are carnivores, while humans and bears are usually omnivores.

options.option_text: Cow
Lion
Eagle
Cat

questions.question_text: If a person chews a piece of bread for a long time, it begins to taste slightly sweet. This suggests that:
questions.explanation: Saliva starts digesting starch in bread into simpler sugars. That is why the taste may become slightly sweet after chewing.

options.option_text: Bread turns into protein in the mouth
Digestion of starch begins in the mouth
Teeth make sugar directly
Bread absorbs oxygen from the tongue

questions.question_text: The process of taking food into the body is called:
questions.explanation: Taking food into the body is called ingestion. It is the first step in nutrition in animals.

options.option_text: Ingestion
Digestion
Absorption
Assimilation

questions.question_text: Humans use lungs for breathing.
questions.explanation: This statement is true. In humans, lungs are the main organs used for breathing and gas exchange.

options.option_text: True
False

questions.question_text: Which organ helps grind and chew food in the mouth?
questions.explanation: Teeth cut, tear, and grind food into smaller pieces, making swallowing and digestion easier.

options.option_text: Tongue
Stomach
Teeth
Nose

questions.question_text: Which tube carries food from the mouth to the stomach?
questions.explanation: The food pipe, also called the oesophagus, carries food from the mouth to the stomach by movement of its muscles.

options.option_text: Windpipe
Oesophagus
Intestine
Artery

questions.question_text: An animal that eats both plants and animals is called:
questions.explanation: Animals that eat both plant and animal matter are called omnivores. Humans are common examples.

options.option_text: Herbivore
Carnivore
Omnivore
Parasite

questions.question_text: Which of the following animals breathes through gills?
questions.explanation: Fish use gills to take in dissolved oxygen from water. Humans and birds use lungs.

options.option_text: Pigeon
Dog
Fish
Lizard

questions.question_text: The breakdown of complex food into simpler substances is called:
questions.explanation: Digestion changes complex food into simpler substances that the body can absorb and use.

options.option_text: Inhalation
Circulation
Digestion
Excretion

questions.question_text: All animals eat the same kind of food.
questions.explanation: This statement is false. Different animals eat different kinds of food depending on their body structure and habitat.

options.option_text: True
False

questions.question_text: A student counts her breathing rate before running and again just after running. Why is the second count usually higher?
questions.explanation: During running, body cells need more energy, so they need more oxygen. The body responds by increasing the breathing rate.

options.option_text: The lungs become smaller during running
The body needs more oxygen during exercise
Food is digested only after running
Running stops gas exchange in the body

questions.question_text: Rahul wants to know whether a fish can live without water for some time like a frog can. What is the best first step?
questions.explanation: To answer safely and scientifically, Rahul should first study how fish breathe through gills and how frogs can use lungs and moist skin in different conditions.

options.option_text: Keep a fish out of water for a long time
Compare how fish and frogs breathe before making any test
Stop feeding both animals
Cover the fish with dry cloth

questions.question_text: If food remains in the mouth only for a few seconds and is swallowed without chewing, what is most likely to happen?
questions.explanation: Chewing breaks food into smaller pieces and mixes it with saliva. Without proper chewing, digestion becomes less efficient at the start.

options.option_text: Food becomes sweeter faster
Teeth digest the food fully
Food is not mixed well with saliva
The stomach stops working forever

questions.question_text: A cow is seen chewing slowly even after it has finished eating grass. This behavior suggests that the cow:
questions.explanation: Cows are ruminants. They bring back partially digested food to the mouth and chew it again. This is called rumination.

options.option_text: is unable to swallow food
is breathing through its mouth only
is chewing cud
is trying to drink water from the air

questions.question_text: Why is it usually advised to eat slowly and chew food properly?
questions.explanation: Proper chewing breaks food into smaller pieces and mixes it with saliva. This makes digestion easier and helps in safe swallowing.

options.option_text: It makes food heavier
It helps start digestion and makes swallowing easier
It removes all water from food
It stops the stomach from producing juices

questions.question_text: A student says, "Digestion starts only in the stomach." Which correction is correct?
questions.explanation: Digestion begins in the mouth, where chewing and saliva start the process. The stomach continues digestion later.

options.option_text: Digestion starts only in the intestine
Digestion starts only after absorption
Digestion begins in the mouth itself
Digestion starts only during sleep

questions.question_text: Which pair is correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Herbivores eat plants and carnivores eat flesh. Goat is a herbivore, while tiger is a carnivore.

options.option_text: Goat - carnivore; tiger - herbivore
Goat - herbivore; tiger - carnivore
Dog - herbivore; deer - carnivore
Cat - omnivore; cow - carnivore

questions.question_text: Why do we call ruminants special plant-eating animals?
questions.explanation: Ruminants are special because they swallow grass quickly and later bring it back to the mouth to chew it again as cud.

accepted_answers: They chew cud and digest plant food in a special way.
They bring back swallowed food to chew it again.

questions.question_text: An earthworm lives in moist soil and takes in oxygen through its skin. What can be inferred from this?
questions.explanation: An earthworm's skin must remain moist so gases can pass through it. Dry skin would make breathing difficult for the worm.

options.option_text: Earthworms use lungs hidden in the tail
Earthworms do not need oxygen
Moist skin is important for earthworm breathing
Earthworms breathe only after rain

questions.question_text: Why do doctors often ask a person with breathing trouble to sit upright rather than lie flat?
questions.explanation: Sitting upright can help the chest expand more comfortably, making breathing easier in many situations.

options.option_text: Because digestion stops in the upright position
Because it may help the lungs expand more easily
Because oxygen can enter only from above
Because the heart stops working when lying down

questions.question_text: Which statement correctly explains the role of the tongue during eating?
questions.explanation: The tongue helps mix food with saliva and pushes it backward for swallowing. Teeth and stomach have different functions.

options.option_text: The tongue digests all food completely
The tongue pumps blood around the body
The tongue helps mix food with saliva and swallowing
The tongue absorbs nutrients into the blood

questions.question_text: How is a fish's breathing different from a human's breathing?
questions.explanation: Fish use gills to take in oxygen dissolved in water, while humans use lungs to breathe oxygen from air.

options.option_text: Fish use lungs and humans use gills
Both use only skin to breathe
Fish use gills in water while humans use lungs in air
Both use the same organ for breathing

questions.question_text: A child places a hand gently on the chest while breathing in and out. What is the main purpose of this activity?
questions.explanation: The activity helps notice that the chest moves during breathing. It is a simple way to observe inhalation and exhalation.

options.option_text: To measure body temperature
To count stomach contractions
To observe chest movement during breathing
To test blood group

questions.question_text: What is the main function of the small intestine in digestion?
questions.explanation: The small intestine completes much of digestion and absorbs digested food into the blood so the body can use it.

accepted_answers: It absorbs digested food into the blood.
It helps complete digestion and absorbs nutrients.

questions.question_text: Seema wants to compare the teeth of a dog and a cow. What is the most useful thing to observe first?
questions.explanation: The type and shape of teeth are linked to food habits. Looking at the teeth helps compare how each animal eats.

options.option_text: The shape and type of their teeth
The color of their fur only
The number of times they sleep
The size of their ears only

questions.question_text: A student records breathing rate in three cases: resting, walking slowly, and running fast. The counts are 16, 24, and 38 breaths per minute. What is the best interpretation?
questions.explanation: As activity increases, body cells need more oxygen and produce more carbon dioxide. Therefore breathing rate increases from rest to fast running.

options.option_text: Breathing rate always decreases with activity
Only walking affects breathing
Greater activity increases breathing rate because the body needs more oxygen
Running stops gas exchange in the lungs

questions.question_text: Why should a person avoid talking or laughing while swallowing food?
questions.explanation: The food pipe and windpipe open in the throat region. If food goes the wrong way, choking may occur, so swallowing carefully is safer.

options.option_text: Because food becomes sweeter while talking
Because talking digests the food too quickly
Because food may enter the windpipe and cause choking
Because saliva stops forming

questions.question_text: A bird has a sharp curved beak and strong claws. Which feeding habit is most likely?
questions.explanation: A sharp curved beak and strong claws are features commonly seen in flesh-eating birds such as eagles and hawks.

options.option_text: Feeding on nectar only
Grazing on grass
Tearing flesh
Sucking mud for food

questions.question_text: Select all correct statements about digestion in humans.
questions.explanation: Digestion starts in the mouth, continues in the stomach and intestine, and absorbed nutrients mostly enter the blood through the small intestine.

options.option_text: Digestion begins in the mouth
The stomach helps digest food
The small intestine absorbs much of the digested food
Teeth absorb nutrients directly into the blood

questions.question_text: If a person's nostrils are blocked during a cold, breathing through the nose becomes difficult. Which chain best explains one effect of this?
questions.explanation: The nose helps filter and warm the air. When nostrils are blocked, air passes less comfortably and breathing feels difficult.

options.option_text: The food pipe closes permanently
Air movement through the normal nasal passage is disturbed, so breathing feels harder
The lungs stop being needed
Blood stops flowing in the body

questions.question_text: Which evidence best supports the idea that fish are adapted for breathing in water?
questions.explanation: Fish have gills that take in dissolved oxygen from water. This is the strongest evidence of aquatic breathing adaptation.

options.option_text: Fish often move their tails
Fish possess gills that extract oxygen from water
Fish have scales on the body
Fish live in groups

questions.question_text:
Assertion: Ruminants can digest grass better than humans.
Reason: Ruminants have a special digestive system that allows cud chewing and better breakdown of plant food.
questions.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains the assertion. Ruminants are adapted for a plant-based diet.

options.option_text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason correctly explains Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason does not explain Assertion
Assertion is true, but Reason is false
Assertion is false, but Reason is true

questions.question_text: How are incisors and molars different in their function?
questions.explanation: Incisors are mainly used for cutting food, while molars are used for grinding and crushing it.

accepted_answers: Incisors cut food and molars grind food.
Incisors are for biting or cutting, while molars crush and grind.

questions.question_text: If an animal has flat broad grinding teeth and no sharp tearing canines, what can most reasonably be concluded?
questions.explanation: Flat grinding teeth are usually associated with plant-eating animals because they need to crush and grind plant material.

options.option_text: It must be a fish
It tears flesh as its main food
It is likely adapted for eating plant food
It breathes only through skin

questions.question_text: Why do some animals have different types of teeth in the same mouth?
questions.explanation: Different teeth perform different jobs. Some cut, some tear, and some grind, making food processing more effective.

options.option_text: Because teeth have no relation to food
Because different teeth are suited for different functions
Because all teeth do exactly the same work
Because teeth are needed only for speaking

questions.question_text: Which is the best explanation for why frogs may survive for some time on land as well as in water?
questions.explanation: Frogs can breathe using lungs on land and also through their moist skin, which helps them survive in more than one environment.

options.option_text: Frogs do not need oxygen
Frogs can use lungs and also breathe through moist skin
Frogs digest food only in water
Frogs have gills throughout life

questions.question_text: A teacher asks students to measure pulse rate before and after climbing stairs. Why might the pulse rate also increase after climbing?
questions.explanation: The heart beats faster to supply more oxygen-rich blood to the body when muscles are working harder.

options.option_text: Because digestion stops completely
Because the heart pumps faster to meet the body's greater need
Because bones produce extra blood instantly
Because lungs are not used during climbing

questions.question_text: Select all statements that are correct about breathing.
questions.explanation: Breathing means taking in oxygen-rich air and giving out carbon dioxide-rich air. It happens continuously and may become faster during exercise.

options.option_text: It involves inhalation and exhalation
It may become faster during exercise
It happens only while eating
It helps the body obtain oxygen

questions.question_text: A lizard catches small insects using quick movement and body control. Which process is most directly helping it stay active during this action?
questions.explanation: The lizard needs oxygen and food-derived energy to stay active. Respiration helps release energy from food for movement.

options.option_text: Germination
Respiration
Photosynthesis
Pollination

questions.question_text: Why is absorption of digested food important for the body?
questions.explanation: Absorption is important because nutrients must enter the blood before body cells can use them for energy, growth, and repair.

accepted_answers: It lets digested nutrients enter the blood to be used by the body.
The body needs absorbed nutrients for energy, growth, and repair.

questions.question_text: An injured bird is breathing fast and appears weak. Which action is the most sensible first response?
questions.explanation: A weak, injured bird should be kept calm and given space and help from an adult or a vet. Stress can make breathing difficulty worse.

options.option_text: Keep it calm in a safe place and seek adult or veterinary help
Force-feed it immediately
Make it fly to test its wings
Leave it in direct sun without water

questions.question_text: Which observation best supports the idea that saliva is useful in digestion?
questions.explanation: Food is softened and mixed with saliva in the mouth, and starch digestion begins there. This shows saliva helps digestion start.

options.option_text: Hair grows after meals
Chewed starchy food may begin to taste slightly sweet
Nails become longer in winter
Eyes close while sleeping

questions.question_text: If a person cannot chew food properly because of tooth pain, which result is most likely first?
questions.explanation: Without proper chewing, food enters the digestive tract in larger pieces and mixes less with saliva, making the start of digestion less effective.

options.option_text: Blood stops flowing to the stomach
Food is swallowed in larger pieces with less mixing of saliva
The lungs digest the food
The small intestine stops absorbing forever

questions.question_text: Which is the best explanation for why many grazing animals spend a long time feeding?
questions.explanation: Plant food is bulky and needs to be collected, chewed, and digested carefully. Many grazing animals also chew cud later.

options.option_text: Because grass contains no useful nutrients at all
Because they do not have a digestive system
Because plant food needs prolonged chewing and processing
Because they breathe through food only

questions.question_text:
Assertion: The small intestine is longer than the stomach.
Reason: A longer small intestine gives more surface and time for digestion and absorption.
questions.explanation: Both statements are true, and the reason correctly explains why the small intestine is long and important in digestion.

options.option_text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason correctly explains Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason does not explain Assertion
Assertion is true, but Reason is false
Assertion is false, but Reason is true

questions.question_text: State one difference between inhalation and exhalation.
questions.explanation: Inhalation means taking air into the lungs, while exhalation means pushing air out of the lungs.

accepted_answers: Inhalation takes air in, while exhalation sends air out.
In inhalation air enters the lungs, and in exhalation air leaves the lungs.

questions.question_text: If an animal has a long sticky tongue and catches insects quickly, what can be concluded about its feeding adaptation?
questions.explanation: A long sticky tongue is an adaptation for catching insects. It helps the animal collect food quickly.

options.option_text: It is adapted mainly for grazing grass
It is adapted only for chewing cud
It is adapted for catching insects
It must breathe through gills

questions.question_text: Select all correct statements about ruminants.
questions.explanation: Ruminants eat plant food, have a special stomach system, and chew cud. Cow, buffalo, and deer are examples.

options.option_text: They are plant-eating animals
They chew cud
They have a special digestive system for plant food
They use gills to breathe

questions.question_text: Why does a person usually feel tired more quickly when breathing is difficult?
questions.explanation: If breathing is difficult, the body may get less oxygen. Since oxygen is needed to release energy from food, tiredness can appear sooner.

options.option_text: Because all digestion stops immediately
Because blood disappears from the body
Because less oxygen may reduce efficient release of energy
Because bones begin to shrink

questions.question_text: A student wants to stay healthy during a long sports practice session. Which choice is the most sensible?
questions.explanation: The body needs energy, water, and proper breathing during sports. A sensible choice supports digestion, hydration, and oxygen supply.

options.option_text: Skip water completely and run continuously
Eat very fast while running
Drink water when needed, breathe properly, and eat balanced meals
Avoid all food for the whole day
